steps.title: Use the scenic context
steps.content: The passage describes lush visual details: shining moss, green pines, cypresses, and willows. “Gorgeous” directly modifies the underlined word, so the word must mean an impressive visible scene.

steps.title: Reject off-tone meanings
steps.content: B suggests pretentiousness, which the admiring passage does not imply. C means similarity, but the passage describes the scene itself. D suggests trickery, which is unsupported. A word meaning a striking sight fits the natural scenery. The answer is A.
